frontmatter.title: Nuclear Image Analysis: Human vs. AI Benchmark

children.value: Welcome to our collaborative research project! This semester, we are benchmarking various image analysis workflows—ranging from manual counting to Deep Learning—to quantify how object overlap (0%–45%) impacts scientific data integrity. You will learn what are the challenges and benefits of each approach.
Your contributions will serve as the foundation for a comparative study on
Each student is assigned a unique ID (
and so on). Your workspace consists of:
: A subfolder for your Phase 4 pixel-wise segmentations.
: Your master reporting table. (Copy the template to your folder and replace the XY by your ID)
Do not change column names or the file structure.
Results from your custom-tuned Fiji macro.
Results from basic global thresholding (Class Workshop).
Results from classic Watershed segmentation (Class Workshop).
WEKA egmantation based - with own sparse training
Number of bounding boxes detected (Phase 5 - Optional).
Perform manual point counting in Fiji/ImageJ.
Establish a “Human Gold Standard” and measure inter-rater variability.
Some images are hidden duplicates to test your individual consistency (Intra-rater test).
Apply global thresholding and Watershed. Compare your own script vs script we make together in class.
Identify the “breaking point” where classic algorithms fail to separate overlapping nuclei. Test limits of reproducibility.
Observe how Star-convex polygons handle the 30% and 45% overlap cases compared to Watershed.
Create precise binary masks for your unique image set.
Name them identically to the source image (e.g.,

Compare detection-only accuracy against full instance segmentation.
Do not “correct” your manual counts after seeing the AI results. We need to document the raw human error.
